==== /.src/tsconfig.json (0 errors) ====
    {
      "compilerOptions": {
        "plugins": [
          {
            "name": "@effect/language-service",
            "effectFn": ["span", "suggested-span", "inferred-span", "no-span", "untraced"]

==== /.src/effectFnOpportunity_pipeWithSpan.ts (6 errors) ====
    import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"
    
    // For these cases we should suggest using Effect.fn (not Effect.fnUntraced)
    // because the piped transformations end with Effect.withSpan. We can extract
    // the span name expression from withSpan and use it as the parameter to
    // Effect.fn("spanName")(function*() { ... }).
    //
    // Using Effect.fn is an improvement because the stack traces will include
    // the call site of that function, not just the span in the traces.
    
    export const arrowWithPipe = () =>
                 ~~~~~~~~~~~~~
!!! suggestion TS377047: Can be rewritten as a reusable function: Effect.fn("arrowWithPipe")(function*() { ... }). effect(effectFnOpportunity)
      Effect.gen(function*() {
      ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
        return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
      }).pipe(Effect.withSpan("arrowWithPipe"))
    ~~~~
!!! suggestion TS377017: This Effect.gen contains a single return statement. effect(unnecessaryEffectGen)
    
    export const functionExpressionWithPipe = function() {
                 ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
!!! suggestion TS377047: Can be rewritten as a reusable function: Effect.fn("functionExpressionWithPipe")(function*() { ... }, ...pipeTransformations). effect(effectFnOpportunity)
      return Effect.gen(function*() {
             ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
        return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
      }).pipe(Effect.map((x) => x + 1), Effect.withSpan("functionExpressionWithPipe"))
    ~~~~
!!! suggestion TS377017: This Effect.gen contains a single return statement. effect(unnecessaryEffectGen)
    }
    
    export function functionDeclarationWithPipe() {
                    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
!!! suggestion TS377047: Can be rewritten as a reusable function: Effect.fn("functionDeclarationWithPipe")(function*() { ... }, ...pipeTransformations). effect(effectFnOpportunity)
      return Effect.gen(function*() {
             ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
        return yield* Effect.succeed(42)
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
      }).pipe(Effect.map((x) => x + 1), Effect.ignore, Effect.withSpan("functionDeclarationWithPipe"))
    ~~~~
!!! suggestion TS377017: This Effect.gen contains a single return statement. effect(unnecessaryEffectGen)
    }
